title: Finance Manager Asia Pacific
description:
Goal of Position

This role will drive key operational and strategic projects within the Finance & IT function, working closely with the Regional CEO and Senior Management Team to ensure financial excellence and business growth.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead and manage operational and strategic projects as well as cross-functional initiatives within the Finance & IT function across the Asia/Pacific region.
  • Support the Regional CEO and Senior Management Team of Finance & IT Asia/Pacific in steering business performance and regional strategic direction.
  • Provide reliable, accurate, and timely financial data to empower the Finance & IT Senior Management team to make value-driven decisions supporting the company’s overall strategic growth.
  • Monitor the execution of action plans and follow-up measures to achieve financial targets and optimize key financial metrics including cost/benefit ratios and working capital.
  • Prepare financial statements, reports, and special analyses across various business levels, including units, divisions, and product lines.
  • Track and report financial progress and target achievement (actual vs. budget).
  • Assist in building and continuously improving effective regional and local finance structures aligned with group processes.
  • Coordinate and support the preparation of comprehensive regional budgets and forecasts, ensuring alignment with business plans and accuracy.
  • Contribute to achieving group financial targets at regional, sub-regional, legal entity, plant, and functional levels.
  • Support monthly closing processes for legal entities within the region.

Job Requirements

  • Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, Business, or related discipline.
  • Minimum 5 years’ experience in matrix organizations and international corporate groups, preferably in industrial manufacturing.
  • Strong analytical and financial modeling skills with meticulous attention to detail and a proactive mindset.
  • Proficient in SAP ERP and advanced MS Office applications.
  • At least 2 years hands-on project management experience managing transformation, change initiatives, or complex transformation projects.
  • Strong understanding of finance structures and group processes to drive effective financial planning, reporting accuracy, and alignment with corporate strategic goals.
  • Excellent business partnering and stakeholder management skills, with ability to translate financial data into actionable recommendations.
  • Fluent English communication skills.
  • Willingness to travel as required.

postedCompany.description:
We Pioneer Motion


As a leading global supplier to the automotive and industrial sectors, the Schaeffler Group has been driving forward groundbreaking inventions and developments in the fields of motion and mobility for over 70 years. With innovative technologies, products, and services for CO₂-efficient drives, electric mobility, Industry 4.0, digitalization, and renewable energies, the company is a reliable partner for making motion and mobility more efficient, intelligent, and sustainable. The technology company manufactures high-precision components and systems for drive train and chassis applications as well as rolling and plain bearing solutions for a large number of industrial applications. The Schaeffler Group generated sales of approximately EUR 14.4 billion in 2019. With around 83,700 employees, Schaeffler is one of the world’s largest family companies. With almost 2,400 patent applications in 2019, Schaeffler is Germany’s second most innovative company according to the DPMA (German Patent and Trademark Office).
postedCompany.name: SCHAEFFLER (SINGAPORE) PTE. LTD.
